description_long: This is a medium-sized, partially open cardboard box with a brown, slightly textured surface. The box features two flaps on the top, one of which is slightly bent outward, revealing a dark, empty interior. On one side, there is a black 'this side up' symbol with two arrows, indicating proper orientation for handling. The opposite side displays a printed message: 'WHY WOULDN'T YOU PUSH THE BUTTON?' in bold, black text. The box is sealed with a strip of tan packing tape along the top seam. The overall appearance suggests it is a standard shipping or storage parcel commonly found in urban environments.
description: A brown cardboard box with open flaps, 'this side up' arrows, and a printed message on one side. The box is sealed with tan tape and has a visible empty interior.
description_view_0: Side view showing the 'this side up' arrows and a partially open flap on the right.
description_view_1: Top-down view with both flaps open, revealing the empty interior and the tan tape along the seam.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with 'this side up' arrows and a closed flap on the left.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the printed message 'WHY WOULDN'T YOU PUSH THE BUTTON?' and the open flaps.
attribute_storefront_signage: WHY WOULDN'T YOU PUSH THE BUTTON?
